hypothesis: On a benchmark of 200 diverse protein–ligand complexes from PDBBind, using a single LoQI-generated low-energy conformer per ligand for rigid-receptor docking will produce a greater fraction of near‑native poses (RMSD ≤ 2.0 Å) than using ensembles of 50 RDKit ETKDG-generated conformers per ligand when both approaches consume equal total docking computational time.
